name: Queen of Pawns
description_short: Florida-based pawnshop chain operating 15 locations since 1995, offering instant cash pawn loans, sell-for-cash, and discounted pre-owned merchandise across jewelry, electronics, tools, and collectibles.
description_long:
Queen of Pawns was founded on March 28, 1995, with its original location in Lake Worth, Florida, and has grown to operate 15 locations across Florida cities including Miami, Tampa, Orlando, West Palm Beach, and Port Saint Lucie. The company positions itself as a sustainable alternative to retail shopping while providing quick liquidity solutions for consumers needing immediate cash.

The company offers three primary service lines: (1) Instant Cash Pawn—collateral-based loans where customers can reclaim items without penalty; (2) Sell for Cash—outright purchase of items the customer no longer needs; and (3) Shop and Save—retail shopping for pre-owned luxury items, jewelry, electronics, tools, and collectibles at 30–60% discounts. They accept a wide range of items including gold jewelry (even broken), diamonds, watches, designer goods, electronics, computers, power tools, lawn equipment, vehicles, motorcycles, and jet skis. The company also operates an online store shipping nationwide and sells through eBay.

Key differentiators include their stated commitment to "the best pawn rates in Florida," explicit no hidden fees and no credit check policies, and free layaway service (10% down, pay in installments). They've implemented digital convenience through a customer portal for tracking transactions, paying pawn extensions, and making layaway payments. Their marketing emphasizes sustainable consumption and community integration, particularly highlighting their 31-year presence in Palm Beach County.

However, consumers should recognize that pawn loans are secured by collateral with inherent risk of item loss if the loan isn't repaid. While the company claims competitive rates, pawn lending terms vary by location and item type, and APR/interest information is not disclosed on the website. The business model depends on item valuation by in-house assessors, which may differ from customer expectations. Additionally, while they emphasize no hidden fees, pawn loans typically involve interest charges that should be clearly understood before engaging.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: No credit check required—accessible to those with poor or no credit history
No hidden fees explicitly stated—transparent about charging structure
Free layaway option with 10% down and flexible installment payments
Operating 31 years with established community presence across Florida
Wide range of accepted items from jewelry and watches to vehicles and jet skis
Digital portal for account management, transaction tracking, and payment extensions
Nationwide online shopping through website and eBay with same inventory discounts
No penalties for walking away from pawn loans—doesn't affect credit score
cons: Interest rates and APR not disclosed on website—customer cannot compare costs upfront
Risk of losing collateral if loan isn't repaid by deadline—fundamentally different from unsecured loans
Item valuations determined solely by in-house pawnbrokers with no independent appraisal option mentioned
Limited to 15 Florida locations—not accessible to customers in most U.S. states for in-person service
Pawn loan terms and redemption deadlines not detailed on public website—requires store visit for specifics
best_for: Consumers needing immediate cash ($500-$5,000+) without credit inquiry or income verification
People with poor credit history who cannot qualify for traditional personal loans or credit cards
Bargain shoppers seeking 30–60% discounts on pre-owned luxury goods, electronics, and tools
Individuals who prefer not to sell items permanently but want temporary liquidity via collateral loan
diagnosis: Queen of Pawns is best for Florida residents or nationwide online shoppers seeking immediate cash without credit requirements, or those looking for deep discounts on pre-owned luxury and everyday items. The main caveat is that pawn loans involve interest charges and collateral risk—if the loan isn't repaid, you lose your item—and specific APR/terms are not disclosed publicly, requiring direct contact with stores for cost comparison.
services: Instant Cash Pawn—short-term collateral-based loans with item redemption option
Sell for Cash—outright purchase of customer items without redemption requirement
Shop and Save—retail purchasing of pre-owned merchandise at discounted prices
Free Layaway Program—10% down, interest-free installment payments with no credit check
Gold and Jewelry Buying—online portal for selling precious metals and gemstones
Online Store—nationwide shipping of inventory items through proprietary website
eBay Marketplace—additional sales channel for pre-owned collectibles and merchandise
Customer Account Portal—transaction tracking, pawn extension payments, and layaway payments
Extended Pawn Options—ability to extend loan deadlines (payment required for extension)
Wide Item Acceptance—purchases and loans for jewelry, electronics, tools, vehicles, collectibles, sporting goods
In-Store Evaluation—professional pawnbroker assessment and competitive cash offers
No-Penalty Loan Cancellation—ability to walk away from pawn without credit impact
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
